---
title: "MISCADA ASML: Classification Summative Coursework Part 2"
output: html_notebook
---

# Model Fitting


```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(mlr3verse)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(skimr)

set.seed(100)
```


```{r}
data <- readr::read_csv("heart_failure.csv")

data_model <- data |>
  mutate(
    comorbidity_count = anaemia + diabetes + high_blood_pressure + smoking,
    fatal_mi          = factor(fatal_mi, levels = c(1, 0),
                               labels = c("Fatal", "Survived"))
  ) |>
  select(-time)

data_model_lr <- data_model |>
  mutate(
    creatinine_phosphokinase = log1p(creatinine_phosphokinase),
    serum_creatinine         = log1p(serum_creatinine),
    platelets                = log1p(platelets)
  )

skim(data_model)
```

Train / Test Split

```{r}
n<- nrow(data_model)
train_idx <- sample(n, size = floor(0.8 * n))

train_data <- data_model[train_idx, ]
test_data<- data_model[-train_idx, ]
train_data_lr<- data_model_lr[train_idx, ]
test_data_lr<- data_model_lr[-train_idx, ]

cat("Training set size:", nrow(train_data), "\n")
cat("Test set size:    ", nrow(test_data),  "\n")
cat("Fatal event rate (train):", round(mean(train_data$fatal_mi == "Fatal") * 100, 1), "%\n")
cat("Fatal event rate (test): ", round(mean(test_data$fatal_mi  == "Fatal") * 100, 1), "%\n")
```

task Definition

```{r}

task_hf <- TaskClassif$new(id= "HeartFailure",backend= train_data,target= "fatal_mi",positive= "Fatal")
task_hf_lr <- TaskClassif$new(id = "HeartFailure_LR",backend= train_data_lr,target= "fatal_mi",positive = "Fatal")
```

Cross-Validation Strategy

```{r}
cv5 <- rsmp("cv", folds = 5)
cv5_lr <- rsmp("cv", folds = 5)
cv5$instantiate(task_hf)
cv5_lr$instantiate(task_hf_lr)
```

Performance Measures

```{r}
measures <- list(
  msr("classif.ce"),
  msr("classif.acc"),
  msr("classif.auc"),# AUC, 1 is perfect, worse then 0.5 bad 
  msr("classif.sensitivity"),
  msr("classif.fnr"),
  msr("classif.fpr")
)
```

Model 1: Logistic Regression

```{r}
lrn_log_reg <- lrn("classif.log_reg", predict_type = "prob")
pl_log_reg  <- po("encode") %>>% po(lrn_log_reg)
```



Model 2: Random Forest


```{r}
lrn_ranger <- lrn("classif.ranger",predict_type= "prob",num.trees= 500,importance= "impurity",keep.inbag= TRUE)# ibag needed to extract each tree OOB curve
```



Baseline Classifier

```{r}
lrn_baseline <- lrn("classif.featureless", predict_type = "prob")
```


```{r}
res_baseline <- resample(task_hf,    lrn_baseline, cv5,    store_models = TRUE)
res_lr       <- resample(task_hf_lr, pl_log_reg,   cv5_lr, store_models = TRUE)
res_rf       <- resample(task_hf,    lrn_ranger,   cv5,    store_models = TRUE)

```
```{r}
baseline_res <- as.data.frame(t(res_baseline$aggregate(measures))) |>
  mutate(Model = "Baseline")

lr_res <- as.data.frame(t(res_lr$aggregate(measures))) |>
  mutate(Model = "Logistic Regression")

rf_res <- as.data.frame(t(res_rf$aggregate(measures))) |>
  mutate(Model = "Random Forest")

results_table <- bind_rows(baseline_res, lr_res, rf_res) |>
  select(Model, everything())

kable(results_table, digits = 3, caption = "5-Fold CV Results") |>
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, position = "center")
```

OOB Error Curve 
```{r}

rf_oob_model <- ranger::ranger(
  formula       = fatal_mi ~ .,
  data          = train_data,
  num.trees     = 500,
  mtry          = floor(sqrt(ncol(train_data) - 1)),
  keep.inbag    = TRUE,
  probability   = TRUE,
  importance    = "impurity"
)

# ranger stores cumulative OOB error: extract by refitting progressively
# using a sequence of tree counts — the standard approach for this plot.
oob_trees  <- c(1, seq(10, 500, by = 10))
oob_errors <- sapply(oob_trees, function(b) {
  m <- ranger::ranger(
    formula    = fatal_mi ~ .,
    data       = train_data,
    num.trees  = b,
    probability = TRUE,
    verbose    = FALSE
  )
  m$prediction.error
})

oob_df <- data.frame(trees = oob_trees, oob_error = oob_errors)
```

```{r}
ggplot(oob_df, aes(x = trees, y = oob_error)) +
  geom_line(colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = min(oob_df$oob_error),
             linetype = "dashed", colour = "tomato", linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(title = "Random Forest OOB Error Curve — Fold 1 (500 Trees)",
       subtitle = "Dashed = minimum OOB error. Plateau confirms 500 trees is sufficient.",
       x = "Number of Trees", y = "OOB Classification Error") +
  theme_minimal()
```

-
Logistic Regression Coefficients
```{r}
lr_fold1_model <- res_lr$learners[[1]]$graph_model$pipeops$classif.log_reg$state$model

coef_df <- data.frame(
  variable   = names(coef(lr_fold1_model)),
  coef       = as.numeric(coef(lr_fold1_model)),
  odds_ratio = as.numeric(exp(coef(lr_fold1_model)))
) |>
  filter(variable != "(Intercept)") |>
  arrange(desc(abs(coef)))   # abs() on the numeric column, not the whole df

print(round(coef_df[, c("coef", "odds_ratio")], 4))

ggplot(coef_df, aes(x = reorder(variable, coef), y = coef,
                    fill = coef > 0)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "tomato", "FALSE" = "steelblue"),
                    labels = c("TRUE" = "Increases Fatal risk",
                               "FALSE" = "Decreases Fatal risk")) +
  labs(title = "Logistic Regression Coefficients — Fold 1",
       subtitle = "Red = increases log-odds of Fatal; Blue = decreases",
       x = NULL, y = "Coefficient", fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal()
```


Random Forest Variable Importance

```{r}

rf_imp <- res_rf$learners[[1]]$model$variable.importance
importance_df <- data.frame(
  variable   = names(rf_imp),
  importance = as.numeric(rf_imp)
) |> arrange(desc(importance))

print(importance_df)

ggplot(importance_df,
       aes(x = reorder(variable, importance), y = importance)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Random Forest Variable Importance — Fold 1",
       subtitle = "Gini impurity reduction summed across all 500 trees",
       x = NULL, y = "Mean Decrease in Gini Impurity") +
  theme_minimal()
```



```{r}

pl_final_lr <- po("encode") %>>%
  po(lrn("classif.log_reg", predict_type = "prob"))
pl_final_lr$train(task_hf_lr)

lrn_final_rf <- lrn("classif.ranger",
                     predict_type = "prob",
                     num.trees    = 500,
                     importance   = "impurity")
lrn_final_rf$train(task_hf)

task_test_rf <- TaskClassif$new(
  id = "HeartFailure_test", backend = test_data,
  target = "fatal_mi", positive = "Fatal"
)
task_test_lr_obj <- TaskClassif$new(
  id = "HeartFailure_test_LR", backend = test_data_lr,
  target = "fatal_mi", positive = "Fatal"
)

pred_lr_test <- pl_final_lr$predict(task_test_lr_obj)[[1]]
pred_rf_test <- lrn_final_rf$predict(task_test_rf)


```


Final Test Set Performance at Chosen Threshold

```{r}
# Reset to default threshold 0.5 for summary comparison
pred_rf_test$set_threshold(0.5)
pred_lr_test$set_threshold(0.5)

metrics <- c("classif.auc", "classif.sensitivity", "classif.ce",
             "classif.fnr", "classif.fpr")

for (m in metrics) {
  lr_val <- pred_lr_test$score(msr(m))
  rf_val <- pred_rf_test$score(msr(m))
  winner <- if ((m %in% c("classif.auc", "classif.sensitivity", "classif.acc")) &&
                rf_val > lr_val) "RF" else
             if ((m %in% c("classif.ce", "classif.fnr", "classif.fpr")) &&
                 rf_val < lr_val) "RF" else "LR"
  cat(sprintf("  %-30s  LR: %.3f   RF: %.3f   [%s]\n",
              m, lr_val, rf_val, winner))
}

cat("\nLogistic Regression:\n")
pred_lr_test$confusion

cat("\nRandom Forest:\n")
pred_rf_test$confusion

```
